name: Saxo Bank
description_short: Danish investment bank offering 40,000+ instruments across forex, CFDs, stocks, and more via its professional-grade SaxoTraderGO and SaxoTraderPRO platforms.
description_long:
Saxo Bank is a Danish fintech pioneer and licensed investment bank founded in 1992, providing professional-grade online trading to retail and institutional clients across the globe. With access to over 40,000 instruments spanning forex, stocks, ETFs, CFDs, options, futures, bonds, and commodities, Saxo Bank stands as one of the most comprehensive multi-asset brokers available. Its platforms—SaxoTraderGO and SaxoTraderPRO—are purpose-built for traders who demand serious tools, market depth, and execution quality.

The SaxoTraderGO web platform delivers a streamlined experience from any browser, while SaxoTraderPRO offers a full-featured desktop client with advanced charting, multi-screen support, and algorithmic trading capabilities. Saxo's OpenAPI enables developers and quant traders to build custom applications, automate strategies, and integrate third-party tools. All accounts include real-time news, market analysis, risk management features, and customizable price alerts across all supported markets.

Saxo Bank uses a tiered account structure—Classic, Platinum, and VIP—where larger balances unlock tighter spreads and lower per-trade commissions. There are no monthly subscription fees; the broker earns revenue through spreads and commissions. Minimum deposit requirements vary by region, typically starting around $2,000 for the Classic tier. Platinum accounts (from approximately $200,000) and VIP accounts (from approximately $1,000,000) receive meaningfully reduced trading costs that make high-volume trading significantly more economical.

Regulated by multiple top-tier authorities including the Danish FSA, the FCA in the UK, MAS in Singapore, and ASIC in Australia, Saxo Bank maintains strong compliance standards and robust client fund protections. The broker is best suited for intermediate to professional traders who can leverage its wide instrument selection and favorable pricing at scale. The steeper learning curve and higher minimum deposit compared to discount brokers may deter beginners, but the platform quality and instrument breadth are difficult to match elsewhere.
affiliate_commission: Revenue share based on client trading volume

tiers.name: Classic
features: Mobile app for iOS and Android

tiers.name: Platinum
features: Reduced spreads from 0.3 pips on EUR/USD

tiers.name: VIP
features: Tightest spreads from 0.2 pips on EUR/USD

pros: Access to 40,000+ instruments across virtually all asset classes
Professional-grade platforms with advanced charting and algorithmic trading
OpenAPI enables full automation and custom integrations
Regulated by multiple top-tier global authorities
Tiered pricing rewards high-volume and high-balance traders
cons: High minimum deposit compared to discount brokers
Platform complexity can overwhelm beginner traders
VIP and Platinum pricing advantages require very large account balances
Inactivity fees may apply depending on region
